title: Conduit architecture artifact
description: A .conduit/architecture.json (or the .conduit/architecture.proposed.json an agent writes for review). A versioned envelope wrapping an ArchDoc — a tree of graphs the app renders as a drill-down canvas. Beyond this schema, the app enforces three referential rules it validates in code (see the skill): graphs[rootGraph] MUST exist or the whole doc is discarded; every edge's source and target MUST be node ids in the SAME graph or the edge is dropped; a node's childGraph MUST be a key in graphs or it is cleared.

updatedAt.description: Epoch milliseconds of the last write. Set to Date.now().

archKind.description: The element's type — drives its color and icon. Any other value silently degrades to "service".

id.description: STABLE, unique id. Never change it across rewrites — edges and the review diff anchor to it.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line qualifier under the title.

description.description: Optional prose. THIS is the field an agent writes narrative/notes into.

childGraph.description: Optional id of the graph this node drills into. MUST be a key in the top-level graphs map or the app clears it.

source.description: Id of the source node — MUST be a node id in this same graph.

target.description: Id of the target node — MUST be a node id in this same graph.

id.description: Graph id. By convention equals its key in the graphs map.

rootGraph.description: Key of the top-level graph in `graphs`. MUST exist there or the whole document is rejected.

graphs.description: All graphs by id. A node's childGraph points at another entry here for drill-down.
